title: Nursing content remediation backlog (execution-ready v1)
auditNote: Priorities align with NurseNest blueprint domains (management_of_care, pharmacological_therapies, etc.), clinical-system buckets, and gap kinds (total, domain, system, rationale, medication, clinical_judgment, lessons). Re-run ops:exam-coverage-audit after DB schema includes nclex_client_needs_category; reorder items using pathway recommendedLessonTopics, recommendedQuestionBankTargets, and rationaleTiers.

scopeMode.shared_core_plus_scoped_overlay: One gold-standard lesson + question spine; pathway overlays adjust scope statements, exam cues, and regional practice language.
scopeMode.pathway_specific: Substantively different objectives or item types for that license track.
scopeMode.country_specific: Regulatory, documentation, units, or public-health framing differs materially (US vs CA).

deliveryMode.lesson_only: Lesson hub depth without a committed question batch (rare).
deliveryMode.lesson_plus_questions_plus_rationale: Lesson + questions + rationale upgrade to strong tier where items ship.
deliveryMode.medication_bundle: Coordinated set across high-alert classes and safety cues (tags/metadata explicit).
deliveryMode.clinical_judgment_scenario_set: Multi-step prioritization / delegation / assignment-style scenarios (management_of_care proxy alignment).

items.topicTitle: Prioritization, delegation, and assignment within LPN scope
items.whyHighPriority: Largest-weight blueprint domain for PN; direct exam readiness for management-of-care items and delegation boundaries.

items.topicTitle: High-alert medications: insulin, anticoagulants, opioids — administration and monitoring
items.whyHighPriority: Medication safety and class breadth are explicit blueprint thresholds; high failure rate on exams when under-taught.

items.topicTitle: Fluids, electrolytes, and IV therapy monitoring (PN scope)
items.whyHighPriority: Cross-cutting physiological content; weak system coverage hits multiple exam items.

items.topicTitle: Infection control, isolation, and PPE sequencing
items.whyHighPriority: Safety domain weight is high for PN; fast ROI with scenario items.

items.topicTitle: Wound care, pressure injury prevention, and skin integrity
items.whyHighPriority: Basic care domain + MSK bucket; common NCLEX-PN stem themes.

items.topicTitle: Diabetes: hypoglycemia, hyperglycemia, and sick-day basics
items.whyHighPriority: Endocrine is a recurring system gap candidate; rationales must teach safe nursing action.

items.topicTitle: Respiratory distress: assessment cues and escalation
items.whyHighPriority: Combines physiological respiratory content with prioritization (who to see first).

items.topicTitle: Mental health: therapeutic communication and safety (PN-relevant)
items.whyHighPriority: Psychosocial domain underweighted in many banks; communication stems are high frequency.

items.topicTitle: RPN scope, accountability, and interprofessional collaboration (Canadian practice)
items.whyHighPriority: Country-specific scope and delegation differ from US LPN framing; reduces learner confusion and item ambiguity.

items.topicTitle: Safe medication practice: MAR, reconciliation, and high-alert meds (Canadian context)
items.whyHighPriority: Medication breadth targets + exam readiness; Canadian documentation and unit literacy.

items.topicTitle: Infection prevention and control in acute and community settings (Canada)
items.whyHighPriority: Safety domain; Canadian public-health framing improves stem authenticity.

items.topicTitle: Older adult care: falls, confusion, and risk reduction
items.whyHighPriority: Risk reduction domain + high-volume clinical scenarios on Canadian PN exams.

items.topicTitle: Heart failure and COPD exacerbation: recognition and nursing response
items.whyHighPriority: Cardio + respiratory crossover; common complex stems.

items.topicTitle: Mental health: communication, stigma-aware care, and safety planning
items.whyHighPriority: Psychosocial integrity weight; Canadian mental-health system context in lesson overlay.

items.topicTitle: Common labs and SI units: interpretation for bedside decision support
items.whyHighPriority: Reduces avoidable errors on lab-heavy items; tag to risk_reduction / physiological_adaptation as appropriate in metadata.

items.topicTitle: Maternal–newborn basics where within RPN scope (regional nuance in overlay)
items.whyHighPriority: Maternity system bucket often thin; scoped items prevent scope creep.

items.topicTitle: Clinical judgment: multi-patient prioritization and unsafe practice recognition
items.whyHighPriority: Highest blueprint weight for RN; directly feeds management_of_care proxy counts.

items.topicTitle: Shock, sepsis, and hemodynamic instability
items.whyHighPriority: Multi-system high acuity; rationale quality matters for learner trust.

items.topicTitle: Electrolyte emergencies (Na, K, Ca, Mg) and ECG linkage
items.whyHighPriority: Classic NCLEX-RN density topic; fills fluids_electrolytes system depth.

items.topicTitle: Anticoagulation, bleeding, and reversal concepts (nursing focus)
items.whyHighPriority: Medication bundle + hematology bucket; high-alert med signals in metadata.

items.topicTitle: Acute coronary syndrome and MI: assessment, interventions, complications
items.whyHighPriority: Cardiovascular system floor driver; pairs with CJ scenarios.

items.topicTitle: Stroke, increased ICP, and neuro assessment
items.whyHighPriority: Neuro system coverage and time-sensitive prioritization.

items.topicTitle: DKA, HHS, and acute glycemic crises
items.whyHighPriority: Endocrine acute presentations; strong question ROI.

items.topicTitle: Pediatric fever, dehydration, and escalation thresholds
items.whyHighPriority: Peds bucket often under-filled vs adult-heavy banks.

items.topicTitle: Canadian acute care clinical judgment: prioritization with Canadian practice language
items.whyHighPriority: Same management domain weight as US RN; overlay reduces US-centric stem bias.

items.topicTitle: Transfusion practice: reactions, monitoring, and documentation (Canada)
items.whyHighPriority: Safety + hematology; rationales must be teaching-grade for legal-sensitive topic.

items.topicTitle: AKI, fluid balance, and diuretic monitoring
items.whyHighPriority: Renal system floor; pairs with fluids/electrolytes cross-links.

items.topicTitle: Obstetric emergencies: hemorrhage, preeclampsia/eclampsia, and stabilization
items.whyHighPriority: High-stakes maternity content; often under-represented vs med-surg.

items.topicTitle: Ethics, capacity, consent, and goals-of-care (Canadian legal-ethical framing)
items.whyHighPriority: Management/coordination + psychosocial crossover; exam-style ambiguity.

items.topicTitle: Airborne/droplet/contact precautions and outbreak principles (Canada)
items.whyHighPriority: Safety domain; Canadian public health context in overlays.

items.topicTitle: GI bleed and acute abdomen: assessment and nursing priorities
items.whyHighPriority: GI bucket depth; complements med-surg volume.

items.topicTitle: Health promotion and screening (Canadian guideline references in lesson layer only)
items.whyHighPriority: Domain weight for RN; avoid stale US-only screening stems in CA pool.

segments.label: US NP (FNP primary; pool shared with AGPCNP/PMHNP until split)

items.topicTitle: Primary care differentials: chest pain, SOB, headache, abdominal pain workups
items.whyHighPriority: NP profile emphasizes differential reasoning and depth; rationale must be teaching-heavy.

items.topicTitle: Polypharmacy, deprescribing, and high-risk drug classes in ambulatory care
items.whyHighPriority: NP pharm weight is highest among blueprint profiles; medication_bundle alignment.

items.topicTitle: ASCVD risk, hypertension algorithms, and lipid management (nursing/NP practice lens)
items.whyHighPriority: Cardio depth for NP-level items; FNP/AGPCNP overlap.

items.topicTitle: Adult preventive care and screening matrix (educational; non-legal)
items.whyHighPriority: Health promotion domain under NP profile; question items test counseling priorities.

items.topicTitle: Psychiatric medications in primary care: initiation, monitoring, referral triggers
items.whyHighPriority: Crosses FNP primary mental health and PMHNP; explicit pathway overlays for depth.

items.topicTitle: Women's health: STI testing, contraception counseling, and urgent red flags
items.whyHighPriority: High-yield ambulatory NP topic; keep FNP-scoped unless expanded to WHNP track.

recommendedBuildOrder.action: Apply DB migration for nclex_client_needs_category; re-run ops:exam-coverage-audit; replace static ranks with audit-driven ordering per pathway.
Upgrade rationales to strong tier on published CJ/management items touched by these lessons.
Ship coordinated high-alert class tagging + lesson spine + question batch in one sprint.
Shared physiological core; CA overlay on labs and documentation where needed.
Reuse cardiovascular teaching where appropriate; NP layer adds outpatient management depth.
Large RN batch; port thin rationale remediation from audit targets.
Fills fluids_electrolytes and peds buckets across tiers.
Batch edit stems/rationales for Canadian context to avoid US-default leakage.
Scope-check RPN items; full RN set for obstetric emergencies.
FNP-first; AGPCNP shares 01/03/04; PMHNP focuses on US-NP-05 expansion.
Schedule by next audit weakest systems per pathway (recommendedLessonTopics).

contentModelRecommendations: Add optional remediation_backlog_id (string) or content_epic_id on pathway lessons and exam questions for traceability from this JSON to shipped assets.
Ensure nclex_client_needs_category is populated on new/edited questions so domain inference matches audit; backfill script keyed by topic/subtopic where safe.
Use explicit body_system and topic/subtopic strings that map to inferClinicalSystemForRow / inferBlueprintDomainForRow to keep audit rollups honest.
For medication_bundle workstreams, require inferMedicationBuckets signals in stems or structured tags so medication breadth metrics move.
For clinical_judgment_scenario_set, tag items that should count toward management_of_care proxy or plan NGN-style case series without changing CAT in v1.
Store country_scope on lesson variants (US_ONLY | CA_ONLY | BOTH) consistent with question region_scope to avoid mixed-context hubs.
Re-export backlog slice per pathway in admin UI by filtering item.pathwayIds contains pathwayId.
